diagrams.title: CNF Production Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the two allowed forms of productions in Chomsky Normal Form
elements: Optional: S → ε (empty string for start symbol)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the binary structure of CNF productions - they either produce a single terminal or combine exactly two variables

diagrams.title: CNF Conversion Process Flowchart
diagrams.description: Step-by-step flow showing the order of CNF conversion
elements: Start → Eliminate ε-productions → Remove unit productions → Remove useless symbols → Convert to CNF form → End
diagrams.purpose: Remember the correct order of operations when converting any CFG to CNF - order matters for correctness

diagrams.title: CYK Algorithm Table Structure
diagrams.description: Dynamic programming table showing how CYK parses a string using CNF
elements: Substrings of length 1 in bottom row
Substrings of length 2 in next row
Each cell shows variables that can derive that substring
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how CYK builds solutions from smaller subproblems using the binary CNF productions

diagrams.title: Parse Tree in CNF
diagrams.description: Binary tree representation of derivation in CNF grammar
elements: Each internal node has exactly two children (variables)
diagrams.purpose: Understand that CNF derivations create binary parse trees - each variable step combines exactly two subtrees
